discussion_comments.comment_body:
**issue(perf)**: Same here, we should keep the old style where `node.useHtmlLabels` is first, so it can short-circuit and potentially improve performance.


```suggestion
  const useHtmlLabels = node.useHtmlLabels || getEffectiveHtmlLabels(getConfig());
```

**issue(perf)**: Same here, we should keep the old style where `node.useHtmlLabels` is first, so it can short-circuit and potentially improve performance.


```suggestion
  const useHtmlLabels = node.useHtmlLabels || getEffectiveHtmlLabels(getConfig());
```

This recursive bit is confusing and draws way too many useless shapes.

I'll try to remove it and only draw the row rects.

That should shave off a great deal of content from the final SVG without altering the look.
It's good to reduce calls to `getConfig()`. Storing the result into a local variable is a good option.
Do not call getConfig() every time, call it once, and then use the value in other places.

@sidharthv96 Understood; we (@Shahir-47 and I) will make sure to update this by calling getConfig() once and then using the value in the other places within the draw() function. As for visual/unit testing, could you please direct us to resources that clarify what kinds of visual/unit tests are expected?

Suggestion: It might make sense to store this `parse` method (or the `services` instance) somwhere, since it's quite costly to recreate the parser instance on every call of `parse`. It of course depends on the amount of calls to this function over the lifecycle of the script.

It should absolutely be saved, as people could have pages with tons of diagrams in there, slowing us down very much. 

---

PS: @msujew your constant support has been truly wonderful and gives me a **LOT** more confidence about the PR. Thank you 😇 

On a related note, do you have any recommended readings for understanding language design, grammars, etc? Apart from one course I took in college (when I had no clue I'd be actually working on grammars), most of my language design learning is ad-hoc. 
But I've read some nasty comments in some discussions in mermaid that the language is "not good". I thought some research into how to do it better is required, before we write some guidelines for developing new diagram syntaxes. 

Maybe we could even update the existing syntax with a simpler, cleaner, and most importantly consistent approach, without breaking changes (falling back transparently to old syntax while parsing).

> Suggestion: It might make sense to store this `parse` method (or the `services` instance) somwhere, since it's quite costly to recreate the parser instance on every call of `parse`. It of course depends on the amount of calls to this function over the lifecycle of the script.

@msujew Fixed. Thanks!
